label: DIRECT_EMPIRICAL
title: Exploration of the Dynamics of Buy and Sale of Social Media Accounts
scope: Observed 38,253 account-sale advertisements across 11 marketplaces from February to June 2024; reported more than USD 64 million in advertised value and a median advertised price of USD 157.
supports: credible or established accounts have observable market value
account attributes and platform categories produce heterogeneous prices
a simple zero-cost identity assumption is false for traded accounts
does_not_establish: cost of producing mutually independent identities

label: DIRECT_EMPIRICAL
title: Uncovering Social Network Sybils in the Wild
scope: Measured more than 100,000 detected Sybil accounts plus 560,000 platform-caught accounts on Renren and analyzed link-creation behaviour.
supports: large populations of Sybil accounts can be produced
Sybil accounts may integrate into ordinary social graphs rather than form isolated clusters
graph-independence assumptions used by some defences can fail

label: DIRECT_EMPIRICAL
title: Smoke Screener or Straight Shooter: Detecting Elite Sybil Attacks in User-Review Social Networks
scope: Analyzed ten million reviews on Dianping and characterized a two-tier market using organically highly rated accounts recruited by organizers.
supports: high-credibility accounts can be recruited rather than fabricated from scratch
labour and market intermediaries can externalize identity-maintenance costs
elite accounts can achieve higher filter-bypass rates

label: DIRECT_EMPIRICAL
title: TROLLMAGNIFIER: Detecting State-Sponsored Troll Accounts on Reddit
scope: Expanded 335 known Reddit troll accounts to 1,248 candidates; reported shared creation timing, suspension and behavioural similarities among many detected accounts.
supports: scaled operations leave correlated behavioural and temporal traces
shared production processes can reduce effective independence
correlation can create detection and correlated-loss risk
does_not_establish: all candidate classifications are true positives

label: DIRECT_EMPIRICAL
title: Socio-Linguistic Characteristics of Coordinated Inauthentic Accounts
scope: Analyzed a five-million-tweet multilingual dataset around the 2017 French presidential election and found coordinated accounts displayed distinctive interaction and temporal patterns.
supports: coordination creates observable cross-account similarities
credible operation requires behavioural differentiation as well as account creation
campaign timing can increase detectability

label: DIRECT_EMPIRICAL
title: Keeping it Authentic: The Social Footprint of the Trolls Network
scope: Modelled social-role footprints in a known influence network and reported approximately 88-91 percent predictive accuracy across evaluated models.
supports: maintaining legitimate-seeming social roles leaves structural footprints
social credibility is a network-level property rather than only an account-level property
increasing identity count may enlarge the surface for correlated detection

label: MECHANISM_SUPPORT
title: Meta disruption reporting summarized in public reporting, 2024
scope: A reported network of hundreds of fake or compromised accounts used AI-generated content; much of it was detected before substantial reach.
supports: content generation automation can lower marginal content cost
large low-quality networks may remain detectable and fail to build authentic reach
account quantity and credible influence are distinct outputs
